author: kevaundray
content:
1) Yep exactly, the io standard is here: https://github.com/eth-act/zkvm-standards/tree/main/standards/io-interface

No-one has implemented it yet, <@1408119112743256134> is looking into implementing it for ZisK

2) Yep thats where we want to have the specs, the format will be a binary format, we are still playing around with what is the most efficient encoding though

author: lukaszrozmej
content: so each client program needs to be able to deserialize that binary format?

author: lukaszrozmej
content: what about standard for precompiles? Is it being implemented? We are trying to integrate ZISK precompiles now and it is a problem for us, due to ZISK doesn't expose something EVM equivalent but there is a rust library in-between

author: kevaundray
kevaundray
content: How I see this playing out, is that we define a `from_bytes` and `to_bytes` that is available to all languages or just use an off the shelf thing like bincode

author: kevaundray
content: Let me know if this doesn't make sense 🫡

author: kevaundray
content: precompiles also have a standard here: https://github.com/eth-act/zkvm-standards/tree/main/standards/c-interface-accelerators

author: kevaundray
content: So the idea is that you would use a C header

author: 0xrubo
content: <@427491045308235776> what we're working on now is the guest app. i believe that host app is a third party that fetches the data from exec client, serializes it and feeds the guest app, right? otherwise, please elaborate. thanks.

author: kevaundray
content: yep this is correct -- I mainly wrote the host to illustrate the workflow, ie the serialization done by the host would need to match the deserialization done by the guest

author: kevaundray
content:
Is there a link to your guest program by chance? I had to do something similar in Golang here: https://github.com/eth-act/skunkworks-tama/blob/eff8e3af014b5a0777f071b73530d64659781b88/tamaboards/serialization/serialize.go#L12

Essentially the `from_bytes` and `to_bytes` methods are defined in Go

author: 0xrubo
content:
our guest is unstable with lots of hardcoded stuff in very early stage here: https://github.com/NethermindEth/nethermind/tree/master/tools/StatelessExecution
and now being heavily refactored here:
https://github.com/NethermindEth/nethermind/tree/feature/stateless-zisk/tools/Stateless/ZiskGuest

author: kevaundray
content: Ah okay so you already have a ReadInput method: https://github.com/NethermindEth/nethermind/blob/fcfd95c60296b7a5719c456ea5ab272e74b8082b/tools/Stateless/ZiskGuest/Zisk.Memory.cs#L15

author: kevaundray
content: I would add a Serialize and Deserialize method, one that works for you and is optimal, then we can look at all of the different impls and standardize on a single one

author: kevaundray
content: bincode is a rust native thing AFAICT, so we may need something more custom

author: 0xrubo
content: i'm not familiar with it but noticed that in zisk docs. worth to consider?

author: kevaundray
content: Since its very Rust specific, I think it may take up quite a bit of your time to get it working (In Rust, we usually put a procedural macro ontop of the structure and it works, which is not possible with C#) -- I would probably reach for MemoryPack and see what the performance characteristic is
